title: fix restart always with slirp4netns
body:
When a container is automatically restarted due its restart policy and
the container used the slirp4netns netmode, the slirp4netns process
died. This caused the container to lose network connectivity.

To fix this we have to start a new slirp4netns process.

Fixes #8047

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
Damn fine work. LGTM

On Tue, May 11, 2021 at 12:54 openshift-ci[bot] ***@***.***>
wrote:

> [APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is *APPROVED*
>

> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCCH3HIXAAKVWXDVFNTTNFOMDANCNFSM44WHLRZQ>
> .
>

Friendly suggestion:
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/500-networking.bats b/test/system/500-networking.bats
index 08026c5f7..5ce28ccce 100644
--- a/test/system/500-networking.bats
+++ b/test/system/500-networking.bats
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ load helpers

     # Bind-mount this file with a different name to a container running httpd
     run_podman run -d --name myweb -p "$HOST_PORT:80" \
+               --restart always \
               -v $INDEX1:/var/www/index.txt \
               -w /var/www \
               $IMAGE /bin/busybox-extras httpd -f -p 80
@@ -67,6 +68,45 @@ load helpers
     run_podman 125 port myweb 99/tcp
     is "$output" 'Error: failed to find published port "99/tcp"'

+    # Tests #10310: podman will restart slirp4netns on container restart
+    run_podman container inspect --format "{{.State.Pid}}" $cid
+    pid=$output
+
+    # Kill the process; podman restart policy will bring up a new container.
+    # -9 is crucial: busybox httpd ignores all other signals.
+    kill -9 $pid
+    # Wait for process to exit
+    retries=30
+    while kill -0 $pid; do
+        sleep 0.5
+        retries=$((retries - 1))
+        if [[ $retries -eq 0 ]]; then
+            die "Process $pid (container $cid) refused to die"
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Wait for container to restart
+    retries=20
+    while :;do
+        run_podman '?' container inspect --format "{{.State.Pid}}" myweb
+        if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
+            if [[ $output == $pid ]]; then
+                die "This should never happen! Restarted container has same PID ($output) as killed one!"
+            fi
+            break
+        fi
+        sleep 0.5
+        retries=$((retries - 1))
+        if [[ $retries -eq 0 ]]; then
+            die "Timed out waiting for container to restart"
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Verify http contents again: curl from localhost
+    # Use retry since it can take a moment until the new container is ready
+    run curl --retry 2 -s $SERVER/index.txt
+    is "$output" "$random_1" "curl 127.0.0.1:/index.txt after restart"
+
     # Clean up
     run_podman stop -t 1 myweb
     run_podman rm myweb
```
Explanation:
* Whenever possible, and not too clunky (this is admittedly borderline), I like to piggyback on existing tests. These are not fine-grain tests for narrowing down the precise failure, they are oh-sh*t slam-bang-it-all tests that should run in as little time as possible and never, ever fail. So here I just tack onto an existing test.
* Your `kill` was a NOP. The busybox httpd ignores every signal I threw at it, even SIGILL. My code above kills, waits for process to die, then waits for process to restart. That code is cumbersome but kind of important.
* I removed the `--network slirp4netns` because these tests are required to run root & rootless, in CI and in gating. Since slirp is the default for rootless, I thought it might be nice to extend the testing to root networking also.

Tests pass with your PR, and fail with `PODMAN=/bin/podman` (i.e., this means that the test really is testing the new code).

/lgtm
(hold is already in place from earlier; feel free to release it once CI finishes)
